title: Self-bootstrapping entry point
description: Create pyxlsx.py at .apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx.py — a self-bootstrapping script that creates a .venv on first run, installs click, openpyxl, and defusedxml, then re-execs itself inside the venv. Follow the same bootstrap pattern as the repo-root pypptx.py.
acceptance_criteria: python3 .apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx.py --version bootstraps a .venv, installs deps, and prints the version
Subsequent runs reuse the existing .venv without reinstalling
Bootstrap script follows the same re-exec pattern as pypptx.py

handoff_summary.completed: Bootstrap script created at `.apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx.py`. Follows exact same re-exec pattern as `.apm/skills/pypptx/pypptx.py`. Syntax verified clean with `python -m py_compile`.
handoff_summary.risks: None — file is new and isolated to the pyxlsx skill directory. No existing files were modified.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should validate the three acceptance criteria: first-run bootstrap, subsequent-run venv reuse, and `--version` output.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used identical structure to `.apm/skills/pypptx/pypptx.py`: `_SKILL_DIR / ".venv"` as venv location, `str(_VENV) in sys.prefix` guard, Windows-aware `venv_py` path, `os.execv` re-exec, then `sys.path.insert` + import after re-exec.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should run: (1) delete any existing `.venv`, run `python3 .apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx.py --version` and confirm bootstrap message + version output; (2) run again and confirm no reinstall message.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Bootstrap requires internet access on first run to `pip install` dependencies.

execution_history.summary: Created `.apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx.py` — a self-bootstrapping entry point that mirrors the `pypptx.py` pattern exactly. On first run it creates a `.venv` under the skill directory, installs `click`, `openpyxl`, and `defusedxml`, then re-execs itself inside the venv. Subsequent runs detect `str(_VENV) in sys.prefix` and skip reinstallation. After bootstrap, the script inserts the skill directory onto `sys.path` and delegates to `pyxlsx.cli:cli`, which already provides `--version` via `@click.version_option`.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
